questions.question_text: Ozone layer depletion results in

options.A: melting of ice at the poles.

questions.explanation: Ozone depletion can cause increased amounts of UV radiation to reach the Earth which can lead to more cases of skin cancer, cataracts, and impaired immune systems. Too much exposure to UV is believed to be contributing to the increase in melanoma, the most fatal of all skin cancers.

questions.question_text: Which of the following factors can affect the boiling and freezing points of water?
questions.explanation:
The boiling point of a liquid depends on pressure, vapour pressure and molecular weight.

Factors affecting boiling point

 Pressure: Boiling point increases as the pressure increases. If external pressure is lower than the atmospheric pressure then the boiling point is less than the normal boiling

questions.question_text: Which of the following diseases in humans is caused by bacteria?
questions.explanation:
Hepatitis B is an infection of the liver caused by the hepatitis B virus . The infection can be acute (short and severe) or chronic (long term). Hepatitis B can cause a chronic infection and puts people at high risk of death from cirrhosis and liver cancer.

questions.question_text: The part of the human ear responsible for amplification of sound is
questions.explanation:
The middle ear 

The ossicles are actually tiny bones — the smallest in the human body. The three bones are named after their shapes: the malleus (hammer), incus (anvil) and stapes (stirrup). The ossicles further amplify the sound.

questions.question_text: The general formula of alkyne is
questions.explanation:
Alkynes are hydrocarbons having a triple bond between the carbon atoms and their general formula is C n H 2n-2 

For ethyne (first member), molecular formula will be:

n = 2

C 2 H 2 x 2 - 2 = C 2 H 4 - 2 

 Ethyne = C 2 H 2

questions.question_text: Which of the following bones is present in the forelimb of man?
questions.explanation:
The forelimb comprises the humerus, radius, ulna, carpals, metacarpals, and phalanges.

Each limb is made of 30 bones.

questions.question_text: Minerals included in the layer ration for egg shell formation are calcium and
questions.explanation: Phosphorus plays a significant role in egg- shell formation. Eggshells contain very little phosphorus (calcium : phosphorus in eggshell is approximately 100 : 1), but this element inter- acts with calcium during bone formation.

questions.question_text: Carrot is a good source of which of the following vitamins?
questions.explanation:
Carrots are second only to sweet potatoes as an excellent source of beta carotene (vitamin A).

 Vitamin A is needed for healthy skin, bones and teeth as well as to fight sickness.

 Carrots are a fair source of B vitamins, like niacin and B6 , which help our bodies use energy from foods.

questions.question_text: The major reason why relative atomic mass of some elements is not a whole number is the existence of
questions.explanation:
The atomic masses of some elements are in fraction instead of whole numbers. This is due to the existence of isotopes having different mass number .

The isotopes of an element have different atomic mass and the percentage of these isotopes vary in nature.

II. High foraging ability

questions.explanation:
Variation is the differences between individuals of the same species, caused by genetic and environmental factors.

A characteristic that changes gradually over a range of values shows continuous variation

questions.question_text: The number of protons in an ion with a charge of +3 is 13. How many electrons are present in the neutral atom?
questions.explanation:
In a neutral atom , the number of protons equals the number of electrons.

Number of protons = 13 = Number electrons

questions.question_text: Metals are normally used as electrical wires because they are
questions.explanation: The physical property of a metal that can be drawn into the thin wire is called ductility or ductility is the property of metal associated with the ability to be hammered thin or stretched into wire without breaking.

questions.question_text: One advantage of male circumcision is that, it

options.A: allows for easy penetration in the vagina.
options.B: keeps the penis free from germs.

The diagram below illustrates a ray of light incident on a plane mirror MM ' .

The value of α is
questions.explanation:
Angle of incidence (α) equals the angle of reflection ( r )

 r + 40° = 90° (right angle)

 r = 90° - 40°

 r = 50°

α = r = 50°

questions.question_text: In which of the following processes is plant nutrient in the soil lost through gravity?
questions.explanation: Leaching occurs when the air spaces in the soil become filled with water and gravity begins to move water downward. The percolating water carries any soluble salts that are present in the soil.

questions.question_text: In which of the following blood vessels in the mammalian body would high levels of urea be found?
questions.explanation: The hepatic vein is connected to the liver. It transports the deoxygenated blood with urea away from the liver to the inferior vena cava. That's why the hepatic vein contains the highest concentration of urea.

questions.question_text: Land breeze occurs when

options.A: cool wind blows across the land during the day.
options.B: warm air rises up from the sea during the day.
options.C: cool wind blows across the sea at night.
options.D: warm air rises up from the land during the day.

questions.explanation:
Land breeze occurs when cool air from the land replace the hot air over the sea during the night.

 Sea breeze occurs when cool air from the sea replace the hot air over the land.

questions.question_text: The following statements are characteristics of large-scale industries except

options.A: volumes of products turned out are large.
options.B: many people are involved in its operation.

questions.question_text: Muscles are mostly made up of
Determine the volume of water required to change the concentration of 100 cm 3 HCl from 0.5 mol dm -3 to 0.1 mol dm -3 .
questions.explanation:
The initial and final moles are the same. Thus the mole doesn't change but only the concentration changes.

Initial molarity = Final molarity

Moles = Concentration x Volume

C 1 x V 1 = C 2 x V 2 

C 1 = 0.5 mol dm -3 

V 1 = 100 cm 3 

C 2 = 0.1 mol dm -3

questions.question_text:
Which of the following statements are functions of soil air?

I. It is for respiration of plant roots.

II. It is for respiration of soil fauna.

III. It is for seed germination.
questions.explanation: Soil fauna includes earthworms, collembolans, mites, nematodes, and protozoa. Microorganisms in the soil and requires oxygen (air) to survive.

questions.question_text: Digestion of food is completed in the
questions.explanation: Digestion begins in the mouth, when you chew and swallow, and is completed in the small intestine.

Which of the following lights will combine with red light to give white light?
questions.explanation: In additive color mixing, red light combined with cyan light, which is composed of green and blue light, will produce white light if combined in the right proportions and intensities.
